description: A designed text/quote card (page 1 of a multi-image set) used by DHS to publish an official joint statement. The layout is on a solid DHS-blue background with red horizontal rules; a large blue-and-white circular quotation-mark icon sits in the top-left corner as a design motif. A centered bold heading reads 'DHS, FBI, FAA & DoD Joint Statement on Ongoing Response to Reported Drone Sightings'. Below, in white body text, are three paragraphs of the statement: noting more than one million drones lawfully registered with the FAA and thousands of commercial/hobbyist/law-enforcement drones flying daily; that the FBI received tips of more than 5,000 reported drone sightings in recent weeks with about 100 leads generated, and that advanced detection technology and trained visual observers were deployed; and an assessment that the sightings to date were a combination of lawful commercial drones, hobbyist drones, law-enforcement drones, manned fixed-wing aircraft, helicopters, and stars mistakenly reported as drones, with nothing anomalous identified and no assessed national-security or public-safety risk over civilian airspace in New Jersey or the northeast. The DHS seal appears at bottom right. This is a designed graphic (branded quote card) rather than a candid photo, though its purpose is informational government communication rather than meme/propaganda imagery. Target audience is the public and press amid the New Jersey drone-sighting concern.
notable_text: DHS, FBI, FAA & DoD Joint Statement on Ongoing Response to Reported Drone Sightings; 'We have not identified anything anomalous and do not assess the activity to date to present a national security or public safety risk'
